diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute decompensation of cirrhosis / portal hypertension
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid ascites is a cardinal feature of hepatic decompensation.
Fluid overload is shared, but does not explain the hepatic stigmata.

symptoms.symptom_name: Orthopnoea / breathlessness from tense ascites splinting the diaphragm

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Mechanical respiratory compromise from tense ascites
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Diaphragmatic splinting by a tense abdomen produces restrictive breathlessness relieved by sitting up.
Altered mental status in cirrhosis with a precipitant (lapsed lactulose) is encephalopathy until proven otherwise.
Jaundice reflects impaired bilirubin handling in advanced liver disease.
Recanalised umbilical portosystemic collaterals are specific for portal hypertension; a lay observer may report bulging veins but formal recognition is a clinical sign.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound weakness, muscle wasting, early satiety and LUQ discomfort

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Advanced cirrhosis with sarcopenia and splenomegaly
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sarcopenia and early satiety/LUQ ache reflect chronic liver disease with mass effect from splenomegaly.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known portal hypertension with prior oesophageal variceal bleed

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: High risk of recurrent variceal haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A prior variceal bleed is the strongest predictor of re-bleeding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cessation of diuretics and lactulose for ~1 week

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Precipitant of ascites surge and encephalopathy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stopping diuretics drives fluid re-accumulation; stopping lactulose allows ammonia to rise and precipitate encephalopathy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile, non-tender abdomen by family account

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever/pain lowers but does NOT exclude SBP, which is often afebrile and painless in cirrhotic ascites.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tense non-tender distension with shifting dullness and positive fluid thrill

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Shifting dullness and a fluid thrill confirm significant ascites — a clinician bedside examination not available in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpable massive splenomegaly (6 cm below costal margin)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Massive splenomegaly indicates long-standing portal hypertension; requires palpation.
Asterixis is a classic sign of metabolic encephalopathy; formal elicitation is a clinician maneuver.

symptoms.symptom_name: Borderline hypotension and tachycardia (BP 95/55, HR 105); tachypnoea RR 26; SpO2 94%

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Haemodynamic fragility / possible sepsis (SBP) and respiratory compromise
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-measured vitals indicating fragility and hypoxia; no connected home device reported, so unobtainable via chat.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute decompensation of cirrhosis with portal hypertension
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Rapid tense ascites + orthopnoea + new encephalopathy + jaundice together define an emergency decompensation, not stable chronic disease.
Caput medusae, varices, gross ascites and massive splenomegaly point specifically to a hepatic (portal) rather than cardiac cause of the fluid.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Mandatory to exclude spontaneous bacterial peritonitis in any hospitalised cirrhotic with ascites; gold standard, inpatient only.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Therapeutic large-volume paracentesis with IV albumin cover
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Relieves the tense ascites causing respiratory compromise; albumin prevents post-paracentesis circulatory dysfunction.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods — FBC, U&E, LFTs, bilirubin, albumin, INR, venous ammonia, CRP
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess synthetic function, coagulopathy, hypersplenic thrombocytopenia, renal function (hepatorenal risk) and sepsis markers.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Upper GI endoscopy for variceal surveillance / banding
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Prior variceal bleed with worsening portal pressures — secondary prophylaxis and re-banding as indicated.
Characterise ascites, portal/hepatic vasculature and screen for hepatocellular carcinoma.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Home diuretic up-titration guided by remote advice
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Remote diuretic adjustment is unsafe here — the patient needs emergency assessment, SBP exclusion and paracentesis, not a home dose change.
